title: androsace carnea subsp. lachenalii
intro: Androsace carnea subsp. lachenalii, also known as the Alpine Rose, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the primrose family. It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe, with small white or pink flowers and hairy leaves. The Alpine Rose has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been used to promote digestive health, reduce stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system.
